generatedSentence: Elias Thorne, a seasoned journalist turned historian, found little peace these days. His old knee often ached, an undiagnosed remnant from years of jogging past the ancient menhir. He stared out at the horizon, a formidable storm brewing, the sky already stormy and filled with unrest. His cozy home was crammed with stuff, including a knobbed walking stick adorned with antlers, and his latest historical masterpiece lay unfinished. The silence before the tempest only amplified his inner discord. On his doorstep, a watchful iguana observed the swaying vegetation. Elias felt like a ghostwriter of his own fate, still undecided on his next move, trying to justify his reclusive life. He knew this feeling, like distant earthquakes, signaled something profound. His only company was the smell of warming ramen. The next morning, the moon still hung low as Elias prepared for a solitary rockclimbing ascent. Suddenly, his antique tuner crackled to life, picking up a distorted satellite signal. Encrypted intel revealed a villainous plot: a notorious hacker, codenamed 'The Terminator' – a heavily built man with a cybernetic eye – was orchestrating a global data hijack. This individual, a former millennials tech magnate whose career Elias had indirectly killed years ago, harbored deep animosity and a terrifying toughness. The broadcast showed a glint of a scimitar and symbols associated with shadowy warlocks. Elias understood the enormity of the threat; this wasn't a game for some loonie. A subtle flick of light in the video revealed a complex cipher, a hidden message for a holder of forbidden knowledge: Elias himself. He couldn't just do nothing. He had to use his unique skill. The dandy appearance of some of the hackers in the video was a stark contrast to their brutal intent. Their plan was to cause a fall of global systems, leading to a worldwide finale. Driven by a powerful mantra, Elias tracked 'The Terminator' to a remote dam in a desolate field of greywackes. A team of manipulated users was already at work, their faces illuminated by lasers from strange devices. The villain, a man in his 40s with a distinctive enamel ring, was lurking nearby, his presence predatory as a jackal. His plan was to emancipate a corrupted digital universe, generating a colossal vapor cloud of stolen information. Elias, with plucky determination, engaged the hacker in a tense dialogue, his movements sometimes jaggy but precise. This outrageous scheme had to be stopped. The hacker lunged, but Elias, a canny veteran, used a quick joint lock, causing a momentary impairment. The battle was no mere moviemaking stunt; it was real, a desperate struggle involving quick thinking and the use of physical icebreakers. Elias even thought he saw a brief glimpse of the hacker's past chickenpox scars, a strange detail in the heat of battle. A momentary flick of the lights added to the chaos. He felt like a lizard adapting to the environment. With 'The Terminator' subdued, Elias prevented further exploitation of the users. A new era of felicity dawned, one built on newfound friendship and digital peace. He watched a breathtaking sunset over the now tranquil dam, the sky painted in hues of aspen and lychees. The experience had been his personal acupuncture for the soul, a profound rebirth. He finally felt truly emancipated. The world, like a dormant volcano, had erupted but found its refuge. He knew his days of being a gnome in his dusty study were over. He'd found a new race to run, a new purpose to lend his wisdom. No more would he misspeak his intentions or feel flaked on by his conscience. He even spotted a majestic dolphin leaping in the distance, a symbol of hope. The quiet hum of a restored satellite network now carried messages of renewed dialogue. The former dockworker in him appreciated the value of honest labor. He saw fields of vibrant sunflowers swaying, ready for new auctions of life. He dreamed of a quiet rowboat ride. This was not a onesie victory; it was a profound shift in his existence.
panelSentences: Elias Thorne, a seasoned journalist turned historian, found little peace these days. His old knee often ached, an undiagnosed remnant from years of jogging past the ancient menhir. He stared out at the horizon, a formidable storm brewing, the sky already stormy and filled with unrest. His cozy home was crammed with stuff, including a knobbed walking stick adorned with antlers, and his latest historical masterpiece lay unfinished. The silence before the tempest only amplified his inner discord. On his doorstep, a watchful iguana observed the swaying vegetation. Elias felt like a ghostwriter of his own fate, still undecided on his next move, trying to justify his reclusive life. He knew this feeling, like distant earthquakes, signaled something profound. His only company was the smell of warming ramen.

The next morning, the moon still hung low as Elias prepared for a solitary rockclimbing ascent. Suddenly, his antique tuner crackled to life, picking up a distorted satellite signal. Encrypted intel revealed a villainous plot: a notorious hacker, codenamed 'The Terminator' – a heavily built man with a cybernetic eye – was orchestrating a global data hijack. This individual, a former millennials tech magnate whose career Elias had indirectly killed years ago, harbored deep animosity and a terrifying toughness. The broadcast showed a glint of a scimitar and symbols associated with shadowy warlocks. Elias understood the enormity of the threat; this wasn't a game for some loonie. A subtle flick of light in the video revealed a complex cipher, a hidden message for a holder of forbidden knowledge: Elias himself. He couldn't just do nothing. He had to use his unique skill. The dandy appearance of some of the hackers in the video was a stark contrast to their brutal intent. Their plan was to cause a fall of global systems, leading to a worldwide finale.

Driven by a powerful mantra, Elias tracked 'The Terminator' to a remote dam in a desolate field of greywackes. A team of manipulated users was already at work, their faces illuminated by lasers from strange devices. The villain, a man in his 40s with a distinctive enamel ring, was lurking nearby, his presence predatory as a jackal. His plan was to emancipate a corrupted digital universe, generating a colossal vapor cloud of stolen information. Elias, with plucky determination, engaged the hacker in a tense dialogue, his movements sometimes jaggy but precise. This outrageous scheme had to be stopped. The hacker lunged, but Elias, a canny veteran, used a quick joint lock, causing a momentary impairment. The battle was no mere moviemaking stunt; it was real, a desperate struggle involving quick thinking and the use of physical icebreakers. Elias even thought he saw a brief glimpse of the hacker's past chickenpox scars, a strange detail in the heat of battle. A momentary flick of the lights added to the chaos. He felt like a lizard adapting to the environment.

With 'The Terminator' subdued, Elias prevented further exploitation of the users. A new era of felicity dawned, one built on newfound friendship and digital peace. He watched a breathtaking sunset over the now tranquil dam, the sky painted in hues of aspen and lychees. The experience had been his personal acupuncture for the soul, a profound rebirth. He finally felt truly emancipated. The world, like a dormant volcano, had erupted but found its refuge. He knew his days of being a gnome in his dusty study were over. He'd found a new race to run, a new purpose to lend his wisdom. No more would he misspeak his intentions or feel flaked on by his conscience. He even spotted a majestic dolphin leaping in the distance, a symbol of hope. The quiet hum of a restored satellite network now carried messages of renewed dialogue. The former dockworker in him appreciated the value of honest labor. He saw fields of vibrant sunflowers swaying, ready for new auctions of life. He dreamed of a quiet rowboat ride. This was not a onesie victory; it was a profound shift in his existence.
rawPanelSentences: Elias Thorne, a seasoned **journalist** turned **historian**, found little **peace** these days. His old **knee** often ached, an **undiagnosed** remnant from years of **jogging** past the ancient **menhir**. He stared out at the **horizon**, a formidable **storm** brewing, the sky already **stormy** and filled with **unrest**. His cozy home was crammed with **stuff**, including a **knobbed** walking stick adorned with **antlers**, and his latest historical **masterpiece** lay unfinished. The **silence** before the tempest only amplified his inner **discord**. On his **doorstep**, a watchful **iguana** observed the swaying **vegetation**. Elias felt like a **ghostwriter** of his own fate, still **undecided** on his next move, trying to **justify** his reclusive life. He knew this feeling, like distant **earthquakes**, signaled something profound. His only company was the smell of warming **ramen**.

The next morning, the **moon** still hung low as Elias prepared for a solitary **rockclimbing** ascent. Suddenly, his antique **tuner** crackled to life, picking up a distorted **satellite** signal. Encrypted **intel** revealed a **villainous** plot: a notorious **hacker**, codenamed 'The **Terminator**' – a heavily built man with a cybernetic eye – was orchestrating a global data **hijack**. This individual, a former **millennials** tech magnate whose career Elias had indirectly **killed** years ago, harbored deep **animosity** and a terrifying **toughness**. The broadcast showed a glint of a **scimitar** and symbols associated with shadowy **warlocks**. Elias understood the **enormity** of the threat; this wasn't a game for some **loonie**. A subtle **flick** of light in the video revealed a complex **cipher**, a hidden message for a **holder** of forbidden knowledge: Elias himself. He couldn't just **do** nothing. He had to use his unique **skill**. The **dandy** appearance of some of the hackers in the video was a stark contrast to their brutal intent. Their plan was to cause a **fall** of global systems, leading to a worldwide **finale**.

Driven by a powerful **mantra**, Elias tracked 'The Terminator' to a remote **dam** in a desolate **field** of **greywackes**. A team of manipulated **users** was already at work, their faces illuminated by **lasers** from strange devices. The villain, a man in his 40s with a distinctive **enamel** **ring**, was **lurking** nearby, his presence predatory as a **jackal**. His plan was to **emancipate** a corrupted digital **universe**, generating a colossal **vapor** cloud of stolen information. Elias, with **plucky** determination, engaged the hacker in a tense **dialogue**, his movements sometimes **jaggy** but precise. This **outrageous** scheme had to be stopped. The hacker lunged, but Elias, a **canny** veteran, used a quick **joint** lock, causing a momentary **impairment**. The battle was no mere **moviemaking** stunt; it was real, a desperate struggle involving quick thinking and the use of physical **icebreakers**. Elias even thought he saw a brief glimpse of the hacker's past **chickenpox** scars, a strange detail in the heat of battle. A momentary **flick** of the lights added to the chaos. He felt like a **lizard** adapting to the environment.

With 'The Terminator' subdued, Elias prevented further exploitation of the **users**. A new era of **felicity** dawned, one built on newfound **friendship** and digital **peace**. He watched a breathtaking **sunset** over the now tranquil **dam**, the sky painted in hues of **aspen** and **lychees**. The experience had been his personal **acupuncture** for the soul, a profound **rebirth**. He finally felt truly **emancipated**. The world, like a dormant **volcano**, had erupted but found its **refuge**. He knew his days of being a **gnome** in his **dusty** study were over. He'd found a new **race** to run, a new purpose to **lend** his wisdom. No more would he **misspeak** his intentions or feel **flaked** on by his conscience. He even spotted a majestic **dolphin** leaping in the distance, a symbol of hope. The quiet hum of a restored **satellite** network now carried messages of renewed **dialogue**. The former **dockworker** in him appreciated the value of honest labor. He saw fields of vibrant **sunflowers** swaying, ready for new **auctions** of life. He dreamed of a quiet **rowboat** ride. This was not a **onesie** victory; it was a profound shift in his existence.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, modern neo-noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: a grizzled, middle-aged man in his late 50s with a weathered face, short grey beard, and a distinctive tattoo of an ancient compass on his forearm, wearing practical outdoor gear. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man sits in a dimly lit, cluttered study, looking out a window at a dark, stormy horizon, holding an open, worn journal, dramatic low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up on the grizzled man's intense, focused face as he stares at a crackling, pixelated screen displaying encrypted symbols and a menacing silhouette of 'The Terminator' (clearly adult male), over-the-shoulder shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man, mid-action, grappling with a powerful, muscular adult male (The Terminator) near a concrete dam, with faint laser beams in the background, dynamic medium shot from below. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The grizzled man stands silhouetted against a vibrant sunset over a peaceful dam, a serene expression on his face, a dolphin leaps gracefully in the distance, wide shot.

definitions.definition: Any disturbed state of the atmosphere, especially as affecting the earth's surface, and strongly implying destructive or unpleasant weather.
A violent agitation of human society; a civil, political, or domestic commotion; violent outbreak.
definitions.example: The proposed reforms have led to a political storm.

definitions.definition: A wind scale for very strong wind, stronger than a gale, less than a hurricane (10 or higher on the Beaufort scale).
A violent assault on a stronghold or fortified position.
Miscellaneous items or objects; (with possessive) personal effects.
definitions.example: What is all that stuff on your bedroom floor?  He didn't want his pockets to bulge so he was walking around with all his stuff in his hands.

definitions.definition: Unspecified things or matters.
definitions.example: I had to do some stuff.

definitions.definition: The tangible substance that goes into the makeup of a physical object.
A material for making clothing; any woven textile, but especially a woollen fabric.
Boards used for building.
Abstract substance or character.
Used as placeholder, usually for material of unknown type or name.
definitions.example: Can I have some of that stuff on my ice-cream sundae?

definitions.definition: Narcotic drugs, especially heroin.
Furniture; goods; domestic vessels or utensils.
A medicine or mixture; a potion.
Refuse or worthless matter; hence, also, foolish or irrational language; nonsense; trash.
A melted mass of turpentine, tallow, etc., with which the masts, sides, and bottom of a ship are smeared for lubrication.
Paper stock ground ready for use. When partly ground, it is called half stuff.
Money.
To fill by packing or crowding something into; to cram with something; to load to excess.
definitions.example: I'm going to stuff this pillow with feathers.

definitions.definition: To fill a space with (something) in a compressed manner.
definitions.example: He stuffed his clothes into the closet and shut the door.

definitions.definition: To fill with seasoning.
definitions.example: She stuffed the turkey for Thanksgiving using her secret recipe.

definitions.definition: To load goods into (a container) for transport.
(used in the passive) To sate.
definitions.example: I’m stuffed after having eaten all that turkey, mashed potatoes and delicious stuffing.

definitions.definition: (pronominal) To eat, especially in a hearty or greedy manner.
definitions.example: She sits on the sofa all day, watching TV and stuffing herself with cream buns.

definitions.definition: To break; to destroy.
definitions.example: He skidded off the road and totally stuffed his brand new car.

definitions.definition: To sexually penetrate.
definitions.example: His wife came home early and found him on the couch stuffing the maid.

definitions.definition: (mildly vulgar, often imperative) Used to contemptuously dismiss or reject something. See also stuff it.
definitions.example: Stuff your stupid rules, I'll do what I like.

definitions.definition: To heavily defeat or get the better of.
definitions.example: Mudchester Rovers were stuffed 7-0 in the semi-final.

definitions.definition: To cut off another competitor in a race by disturbing his projected and committed racing line (trajectory) by an abrupt manoeuvre.
definitions.example: I got stuffed by that guy on the supermoto going into that turn, almost causing us to crash.

definitions.definition: To preserve a dead bird or other animal by filling its skin.
To obstruct, as any of the organs; to affect with some obstruction in the organs of sense or respiration.
To form or fashion by packing with the necessary material.
To crowd with facts; to cram the mind of; sometimes, to crowd or fill with false or idle tales or fancies.
To compress (a file or files) in the StuffIt format, to be unstuffed later.
The absence of any sound.
definitions.example: When the motor stopped, the silence was almost deafening.

definitions.definition: The act of refraining from speaking.
definitions.example: "You have the right to silence," said the police officer.

definitions.definition: Form of meditative worship practiced by the Society of Friends (Quakers); meeting for worship.
definitions.example: During silence a message came to me that there was that of God in every person.

definitions.definition: To make (someone or something) silent.
definitions.example: Can you silence the crowd, so we can start the show?

definitions.definition: To repress the expression of something.
definitions.example: Women, as well as children, have their thoughts or emotions routinely silenced.

definitions.definition: To suppress criticism, etc.
definitions.example: Silence the critics.

definitions.definition: To block gene expression.
Murder
Be silent.
definitions.example: Silence! Enough of your insolence!

definitions.definition: A moon or other smaller body orbiting a larger one.
definitions.example: A spent upper stage is a derelict satellite.

definitions.definition: A man-made apparatus designed to be placed in orbit around a celestial body, generally to relay information, data etc. to Earth.
definitions.example: Many telecommunication satellites orbit at 36000km above the equator.

definitions.definition: A country, state, office, building etc. which is under the jurisdiction, influence, or domination of another body.
An attendant on an important person; a member of someone's retinue, often in a somewhat derogatory sense; a henchman.
Satellite TV; reception of television broadcasts via services that utilize man-made satellite technology.
definitions.example: Do you have satellite at your house?

definitions.definition: (grammar) A grammatical construct that takes various forms and may encode a path of movement, a change of state, or the grammatical aspect. Examples: "a bird flew past"; "she turned on the light".
To transmit by satellite.
Of or pertaining to storms.
Characterized by, or proceeding from, a storm; subject to storms; agitated with strong winds and heavy rain.
definitions.example: a stormy season or a stormy day

definitions.definition: Proceeding from violent agitation or fury.
definitions.example: a stormy sound or stormy shocks

definitions.definition: Violent; passionate; rough.
definitions.example: stormy passions

definitions.definition: The sport of scaling rock faces, either with or without ropes and other equipment.
Soup noodles of wheat, with various ingredients (Japanese style)
A type of instant noodles.
(Pastafarianism) amen
A small open boat propelled by oars (by rowing).
To break or chip off in a flake.
definitions.example: The paint flaked off after only a year.

definitions.definition: To prove unreliable or impractical; to abandon or desert, to fail to follow through.
definitions.example: He said he'd come and help, but he flaked.

definitions.definition: To store an item such as rope or sail in layers
definitions.example: The line is flaked into the container for easy attachment and deployment.

definitions.definition: To hit (another person).
A branching and bony structure on the head of deer, moose and elk, normally in pairs. They are grown and shed each year. (Compare with horn, which is generally not shed.)
definitions.example: While hiking in the woods, I found an antler from a deer.

definitions.definition: Lack of concord, agreement or harmony.
Tension or strife resulting from a lack of agreement; dissension.
An inharmonious combination of simultaneously sounded tones; a dissonance.
Any harsh noise, or confused mingling of sounds.
To disagree; to fail to agree or harmonize; clash.
In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.
definitions.example: Penny was wearing a miniskirt, so she skinned her exposed knees when she fell.

definitions.definition: In the horse and allied animals, the carpal joint, corresponding to the wrist in humans.
The part of a garment that covers the knee.
A piece of timber or metal formed with an angle somewhat in the shape of the human knee when bent.
An act of kneeling, especially to show respect or courtesy.
definitions.example: To make a knee.

definitions.definition: Any knee-shaped item or sharp angle in a line; an inflection point.
definitions.example: the knee of a graph

definitions.definition: A blow made with the knee; a kneeing.
To kneel to.
definitions.example: 1605: I could as well be brought / To knee his throne and, squire-like, pension beg / To keep base life afoot. — William Shakespeare, King Lear II.ii

definitions.definition: To poke or strike with the knee.
To move on the knees; to use the knees to move.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(of steel) deoxidized
The Canadian dollar (both the coin and the monetary unit).
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
A thing that holds.
definitions.example: Put your umbrella in the umbrella holder.

definitions.definition: A person who temporarily or permanently possesses something.
definitions.example: He's been an account holder with us since 2004.

definitions.definition: One who is employed in the hold of a vessel.
The defending champion.
A ship designed to break through ice so that it, or other ships coming behind, can navigate on frozen seas.
definitions.example: The steel hulls of ice-breakers are much thicker than those of standard vessels.

definitions.definition: A game, activity, humorous anecdote, etc., designed to relax a group of people to help them get to know each other.
definitions.example: The new college hallmates were awkward with each other at first, but after a game of charades as an icebreaker, they were laughing like old friends.

definitions.definition: A lively song and dance routine at the start of a musical.
The Earth's moon Luna; the sole natural satellite of the Earth, represented in astronomy and astrology by ☾.
The god of the Moon in Heathenry.
A surname.
The 54th sura (chapter) of the Qur'an.
(by extension of Moon) Any natural satellite of a planet.
definitions.example: That's no moon, you idiot... it's a space station!

definitions.definition: A month, particularly a lunar month.
definitions.example: They stayed with their aunt and uncle for many moons.

definitions.definition: A crescent-like outwork in a fortification.
definitions.example: The moons surrounding the city walls were built in the sixteenth century.

definitions.definition: The eighteenth trump/major arcana card of the Tarot.
The thirty-second Lenormand card.
In hearts, the action of taking all the point cards in one hand.
To display one's buttocks to, typically as a jest, insult, or protest.
definitions.example: It was ill-advised of Sam to moon the photographer during the shoot.

definitions.definition: (usually followed by over or after) To fuss over something adoringly; to be infatuated with someone.
definitions.example: Sarah mooned over Sam's photograph for months.

definitions.definition: To spend time idly, absent-mindedly.
To expose to the rays of the Moon.
To adorn with moons or crescents.
(cryptocurrency) Of a coin or token: to rise in price rapidly.
definitions.example: It is impractical if a currency moons and plummets often.

definitions.definition: To shoot the moon.
Capacity to do something well; technique, ability. Skills are usually acquired or learned, as opposed to abilities, which are often thought of as innate.
Discrimination; judgment; propriety; reason; cause.
Knowledge; understanding.
Display of art; exercise of ability; contrivance; address.
Great, excellent.
A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.
definitions.example: Our lounge strives to maintain an environment of peace for the comfort of our customers.

definitions.definition: A state free of oppressive and unpleasant thoughts and emotions.
definitions.example: The safety equipment will give me some peace of mind.

definitions.definition: Harmony in personal relations.
A state free of war, in particular war between different countries.
definitions.example: May there be peace in our time.

definitions.definition: To make peace; to put at peace; to be at peace.
To peace out.
Shut up!, silence!; be quiet, be silent.
Shortened form of peace out; goodbye.
A person who works on the dock of a harbor or shipyard, usually employed to load or unload freight.
A person who tunes a piano or organ.
A device, electronic or mechanical, that helps a person tune a musical instrument by showing the deviation of the played pitch from the desired pitch.
On a musical instrument, a peg or mechanical device that changes the tension, and hence pitch, of a string.
The component of an audio system that receives radio broadcasts.
One who sings or makes music.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
To move briskly, especially as a dance.
definitions.example: The guests were jigging around on the dance floor.

definitions.definition: To move with a skip or rhythm; to move with vibrations or jerks.
To fish with a jig.
To sing to the tune of a jig.
To trick or cheat; to cajole; to delude.
To sort or separate, as ore in a jigger or sieve.
To cut or form, as a piece of metal, in a jigging machine.
A short, quick movement, especially a brush, sweep, or flip.
definitions.example: He removed the speck of dust with a flick of his finger.

definitions.definition: A motion picture; (in plural, usually preceded by "the") movie theater, cinema.
definitions.example: My all-time favorite flick is "Gone with the Wind."

definitions.definition: A cut that lands with the point, often involving a whip of the foible of the blade to strike at a concealed target.
A powerful underarm volley shot.
The act of pressing a place on a touch screen device.
A flitch.
definitions.example: a flick of bacon

definitions.definition: A unit of time, equal to 1/705,600,000 of a second
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: A hard dark sandstone with poorly sorted angular grains of quartz, feldspar, and small rock fragments in a compact, clay-fine matrix.
(of a disease or condition) That had not been diagnosed

To cut into a surface with an acid or other corrosive substance in order to make a pattern. Best known as a technique for creating printing plates, but also used for decoration on metal, and, in modern industry, to make circuit boards.
To engrave a surface.
To make a lasting impression.
definitions.example: The memory of 9/11 is etched into my mind.

definitions.definition: To sketch; to delineate.
The art of producing an image from a metal plate into which an image or text has been etched with acid.
The image created by this process.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.
definitions.example: She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.
One whose occupation is journalism, originally only writing in the printed press.

An unctuous person.
A coward.

(rock paper scissors) A hand forming a "D" shape with the tips of the thumb and index finger touching (a handshape resembling a lizard), that beats paper and Spock and loses to rock and scissors in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
(in compounds) A person who idly spends time in a specified place, especially a promiscuous female.
definitions.example: lounge lizard; lot lizard; beach lizard; truck stop lizard

definitions.definition: Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

definitions.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

definitions.definition: An entity similar to our universe; one component of a larger entity known as the multiverse.
Everything under consideration.
definitions.example: In all this universe of possibilities, there is only one feasible option.

definitions.definition: A sample taken from the population.
An imaginary collection of worlds.
definitions.example: The universe in this comic book series is richly imagined.

definitions.definition: (films) A collection of stories with characters and settings that are less interrelated than those of sequels or prequels.
A whole world, in the sense of perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: That didn’t just rock my world, it rocked my universe.

definitions.definition: Plants, taken collectively.
definitions.example: There were large amounts of vegetation in the forest.

definitions.definition: An abnormal verrucous or fibrinous growth
The act or process of vegetating, or growing as a plant does; vegetable growth.
